title: pimpinella tragium subsp. glauca
intro: Pimpinella tragium subsp. glauca, also known as burnet saxifrage, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its content of essential oils and flavonoids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The plant has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, and has also been reported to have anti-cancer and anti-bacterial properties. Herbal preparations of burnet saxifrage can be made through infusion, decoction, or tincture, and are often used to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
